zotero.abstractNote: We propose a novel method for generating synthetic data under differential privacy. Our approach relies on approximating the data distribution using a truncated expansion of Hermite polynomials. This allows us to capture complex dependencies in the data while maintaining a low-dimensional representation, which is crucial for privacy. We provide theoretical guarantees on the privacy loss and the approximation error. Our empirical results demonstrate that our method outperforms existing state-of-the-art approaches on several benchmark datasets, especially in high-dimensional settings.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 39th International Conference on Machine Learning
